04sponsored
Securing the service desk: Why social engineering attacks keep succeeding
bleepingcomputer.com · 2026-06-24 14:02 openai / deepseek-v4-pro
TAGScarnival-corporationco-opdragonforcefbiharrodsmarks-and-spencermfa-bypassscattered-spidersilent-ransom-groupsim-swappingsocial-engineeringspecops-secure-service-deskspecops-softwareunited-kingdomvishing
50
In plain words

The article explains how criminals trick company service desk workers into resetting passwords or login checks by pretending to be real employees, as seen in attacks involving Marks & Spencer, Co-op, Harrods, and Carnival Corporation. This affects companies, their employees, and outside support providers because one convincing phone call or chat can give attackers access to internal systems. The stakes are high because that access can lead to stolen data, locked computer systems, and extortion.

Summary
Service desks remain a high-value target for social engineering because attackers can trick support staff into resetting credentials or MFA, gaining access faster than by breaking technical defenses. Organizations should harden identity verification, limit help desk privileges, monitor reset activity, and train staff against phone and chat-based impersonation.

Service desk social engineering remains one of the most effective ways for attackers to gain access to corporate systems. The article highlights 2025 attacks against UK retailers Marks & Spencer, Co-op, and Harrods by Scattered Spider, and says these tactics are not isolated. In the Marks & Spencer case, Chairman Archie Norman confirmed that attackers impersonated an employee and convinced a third-party service desk agent to reset credentials, giving access to internal systems.

The article explains that attackers target service desks because they are high-leverage, low-resistance entry points into corporate networks. Service desk staff are trained to help and may be pressured by urgent, knowledgeable-sounding callers, while agents often have the ability to reset passwords, provision accounts, or disable multi-factor authentication. The article says this lets attackers bypass technical defenses and gain access quickly, often without triggering security alerts.

A typical service desk attack described in the article includes reconnaissance, impersonation, credential reset or MFA bypass, access and lateral movement, and then ransomware or data theft. Attackers may use LinkedIn, org charts, data leaks, VoIP spoofing, SIM-swapped phones, and internal-sounding language to make requests appear legitimate. The article recommends strict identity verification, stronger controls for MFA resets, service desk training, monitoring for unusual reset activity, limits on help desk privileges, and regular reviews of outsourced service desk procedures.

  • Attackers do not need to defeat technical defenses if they can persuade a service desk agent to use legitimate account recovery processes on their behalf.
  • Outsourced or decentralized IT support is a particular concern because the article cites large companies with those support models as common targets and recommends regularly testing outsourced verification procedures.
  • Caller ID and internal knowledge are weak verification signals because attackers may spoof internal numbers and use details from LinkedIn, org charts, ticketing systems, or data leaks to sound legitimate.
  • MFA can become a point of failure when service desk staff are allowed to remove or re-register it without stronger checks such as in-person verification, manager approval, or out-of-band confirmation.
  • Monitoring help desk actions such as repeated password resets or MFA removals is presented as important because a successful social engineering call may not trigger conventional security alerts.
Key Points
  • 01Service desk social engineering remains one of the most effective ways for attackers to gain access to corporate systems.
  • 02In the Marks & Spencer case, Chairman Archie Norman confirmed that attackers impersonated an employee and convinced a third-party service desk agent to reset credentials, providing access to internal systems.
  • 03Attackers target service desks because staff can reset passwords, provision accounts, or disable multi-factor authentication, giving attackers a direct path to legitimate access.
  • 04A typical service desk attack can involve reconnaissance, impersonation, credential reset or MFA bypass, access and lateral movement, and eventual ransomware deployment or data theft.
  • 05The article recommends strict identity verification, stronger MFA reset controls, service desk training, monitoring of reset activity, limited help desk privileges, and regular reviews of outsourced service desk arrangements.
OPEN SOURCE ↗
JUSTIFICATION

This is a sponsored best-practice article about service desk social engineering rather than a report on a new incident or technical finding. It references named attacks and threat groups, but its main purpose is evergreen guidance and product promotion, so it is lower priority for security professionals.

Insufficient Egress Filtering: How Weak Outbound Controls Enable Attacks
blackhillsinfosec.com · 2026-06-24 14:00 openai / deepseek-v4-pro
TAGScommand-and-controldata-exfiltrationdns-tunnelingegress-filtering
45
In plain words

The article is about how weak checks on information leaving a network can make attacks easier. Organizations that do not control outgoing connections are affected, because attackers may be able to use those open paths once they get inside. The broader risk is that a small weakness in outbound controls can help turn an intrusion into a more serious real-world security incident.

Summary
Weak egress filtering lets compromised systems freely communicate with attacker infrastructure and exfiltrate data. Organizations should tightly control and monitor outbound traffic to reduce command-and-control, lateral movement, and data theft risks.

The article is titled “Insufficient Egress Filtering: How Weak Outbound Controls Enable Attacks.” It focuses on egress filtering, meaning controls over outbound activity. The central point presented is that weak outbound controls can enable attacks.

The significance of the article is its emphasis on outbound controls as a security concern. Rather than describing a specific named incident, organization, threat actor, or product, the readable information frames insufficient egress filtering as a condition that helps attacks succeed. The key technical detail available is the weakness of controls governing traffic or activity leaving an environment.

  • The article emphasizes that traffic leaving a network deserves security attention, not only traffic coming in.
  • The article presents weak outbound controls as a practical weakness that can make attacks easier to carry out.
  • The article implies that improving egress filtering is an actionable way to reduce attack opportunities.
Key Points
  • 01The article is about insufficient egress filtering.
  • 02The article describes weak outbound controls as a factor that can enable attacks.
  • 03The article focuses on controls for traffic leaving a network.
OPEN SOURCE ↗
JUSTIFICATION

The article appears to cover the general concept of insufficient egress filtering and weak outbound controls rather than a specific incident, advisory, named threat actor operation, or concrete vulnerability. As evergreen educational or best-practice content, it is useful but low priority under the scoring rules.

06sponsored
Dawn of the Apex Agentic Adversary
thehackernews.com · 2026-06-24 11:30 openai / deepseek-v4-pro
TAGSbacnetcisa-known-exploited-vulnerabilities-catalogepssmodbusrunzeros7comm
35
In plain words

The article says new AI tools are starting to find and use weaknesses in computer systems much faster than people can respond. Companies that connect office networks, factory equipment, internal tools, and outside AI services could be affected because hidden devices or weak connections may give attackers a path in. The real-world risk is that a break-in may not just expose data, but could also disrupt physical operations like factory floors or safety systems.

Summary
The article warns that agentic AI will drastically compress the time from vulnerability discovery to exploitation, making traditional catalog- and signature-based defense less effective. It argues organizations must proactively map assets, validate segmentation, and close IT/OT blind spots before automated adversaries exploit them.

The article argues that cybersecurity is nearing the end of an era of “human-speed threats” as frontier agentic AI models emerge in early 2026. It says these models no longer only suggest code, but actively test it, compressing the time between discovery and weaponization. The article warns that attackers could find an exposure, weaponize it, and execute a breach before human defenders can respond.

It highlights that organizations have given AI deep access to infrastructure, including write access to code repositories and third-party AI wrappers connected to internal APIs. The article says AI-driven attacks may become so fast, targeted, and constantly changing that public tracking systems such as CISA’s KEV Catalog and EPSS may not capture them effectively. It also warns that converged IT and OT environments create paths for AI attackers to move from corporate systems into industrial networks.

The article identifies blind spots such as rogue access points, forgotten IoT devices, shadow IT, multi-homed devices, technician laptops, and assets hidden behind protocol gateways as major risks. It describes industrial protocols such as Modbus, BACnet, and S7comm as routes attackers could use in OT environments. It presents runZero’s asset discovery, segmentation validation, attack path mapping, and risk prioritization capabilities as ways to expose hidden assets, validate assumptions, and identify choke points before adversaries exploit them.

  • The article frames AI-enabled productivity integrations as a defensive liability because the same access that lets them modify code and connect to internal APIs can deepen the impact of a compromised or misused tool.
  • The article suggests that defenders should focus less on fixing every exposure equally and more on identifying choke points where vulnerabilities overlap with real paths into critical systems.
  • The article highlights multi-homed devices and technician laptops as especially important blind spots because they can unintentionally bridge corporate networks and factory environments.
  • The article argues that unauthenticated asset discovery matters because assets without installed agents or credentials may be exactly the systems an automated adversary can use as an entry point.
Key Points
  • 01The article argues that frontier agentic AI models emerging in early 2026 can actively test code and compress the time between vulnerability discovery and weaponization.
  • 02The article says organizations have given AI tools deep access to infrastructure, including write access to code repositories and connections to internal APIs through third-party AI wrappers.
  • 03The article states that AI-driven breaches may become fast, targeted, and constantly changing enough that public tracking systems such as CISA’s KEV Catalog and EPSS may not capture them effectively.
  • 04The article warns that converged IT and OT environments can allow AI attackers to move from corporate systems into industrial systems through bridging devices and insecure-by-design protocols such as Modbus, BACnet, and S7comm.
  • 05The article presents runZero’s asset discovery, segmentation validation, attack path mapping, and risk prioritization capabilities as ways to reduce network blind spots and identify critical exposure paths.
OPEN SOURCE ↗
JUSTIFICATION

This is a sponsored trend/opinion piece about agentic AI risks and IT/OT visibility rather than a report on a specific incident, vulnerability, patch, or threat actor operation. It may be conceptually relevant, but it is low priority for security professionals seeking actionable current intelligence.

↑ TOP

In this article

Glossary
.NET DLL concept
A dynamically linked library containing .NET code, which can be loaded and executed by applications.
2G technology protocol
2G technology is an older second-generation mobile network technology used for basic calls, texts, and low-speed data.
403s concept
HTTP 403 status codes signifying that access to a resource is forbidden.
5G technology protocol
5G technology is the fifth generation of mobile network technology, offering faster speeds and lower delays than earlier generations.
800 terabytes concept
800 terabytes is a very large amount of digital data storage, equal to about 800,000 gigabytes.
8Base threat-actor
8Base is a ransomware and data-extortion group known for targeting organizations and leaking stolen data.
_Block_signature concept
_Block_signature is a low-level Objective-C block metadata field related to how function-like code blocks are represented.
access control bypass vulnerability
Access control bypass is a flaw or technique that lets someone reach data or functions they should not be allowed to use.
access token concept
A digital credential that grants temporary access to a protected resource or API on behalf of a user.
ACH protocol
ACH is a U.S. electronic bank transfer network commonly used for payroll, bill payments, and direct deposits.
action.yml concept
action.yml is the metadata file that defines how a GitHub Action works and what inputs or commands it uses.
active-directory
Active Directory is Microsoft’s system for managing users, computers, and permissions in many corporate networks.
adk-samples product
adk-samples is a sample code repository name, likely containing examples for an Agent Development Kit.
AES-128 protocol
AES-128 is a widely used encryption method that protects data with a 128-bit secret key.
AES-128-GCM protocol
AES-128-GCM is an encryption method that uses AES with a 128-bit key and also checks that data was not tampered with.
AES-256-CBC protocol
AES-256-CBC is an encryption method that uses the AES algorithm with a 256-bit key in CBC mode.
Agentic AI concept
Agentic AI is artificial intelligence designed to take actions and pursue goals with limited human step-by-step control.
agentic tools concept
Autonomous software tools powered by large language models that can perform tasks and make decisions with minimal human guidance.
agentless-first visibility concept
Agentless-first visibility is a security approach that monitors systems mainly through existing APIs and logs before installing software agents on devices.
AI concept
AI, or artificial intelligence, is technology that can perform tasks that normally require human reasoning or decision-making.
AI Agent Development Kit tool
AI Agent Development Kit is a software toolkit for building AI agents that can perform tasks and use tools.
AI coding assistant tool
An AI coding assistant is a tool that helps developers write, explain, edit, or run code using artificial intelligence.
AI Medical Device Safety concept
AI Medical Device Safety is the practice of making sure AI-enabled medical devices are safe, reliable, and protected from misuse or cyber risks.
ai-coding-assistant-hijacking technique
AI coding assistant hijacking is when an attacker manipulates an AI coding tool or its settings so it suggests or runs harmful code.
AI-related security issues concept
Security risks that arise from the use or misuse of artificial intelligence technologies.
Aikido product
A security platform that scans applications, cloud infrastructure, and code repositories for vulnerabilities and misconfigurations.
air gap concept
The physical isolation of a computer or network from unsecured networks, like the internet, to prevent remote attacks.
Akira threat-actor
Akira is a ransomware and extortion group that attacks organizations and threatens to leak stolen data.
Amadey malware
Amadey is a malware bot that can collect system information and download additional malware onto infected computers.
anthropic
Anthropic is an AI company known for the Claude family of AI assistants.
Anthropic Skills product
Anthropic Skills are packaged capabilities or instructions that extend what Anthropic’s AI assistant can do for specific tasks.
anti-forensic tactics technique
Anti-forensic tactics are actions attackers take to hide evidence, erase traces, or make investigations harder.
anti-forensics technique
Anti-forensics is the practice of hiding, destroying, or confusing evidence so investigators cannot easily reconstruct what happened.
Apache organization
Apache is a software foundation and brand behind many widely used open-source projects, including web and data tools.
Apache Doris product
Apache Doris is an open-source database system used for fast analytics on large amounts of data.
Apfell tool
Apfell is the earlier name for Mythic, a command-and-control framework used in security testing and potentially by attackers.
API hooks technique
API hooks are ways to intercept or change how software functions are called, often used for monitoring but also by malware.
API keys concept
API keys are secret codes used to let software access an online service.
API tokens concept
API tokens are digital keys used to let software access an application or service.
APIs protocol
APIs are interfaces that let software applications request data or actions from each other in a structured way.
AppleScript tool
AppleScript is a macOS scripting language used to automate applications and system tasks.
APT73 threat-actor
An advanced persistent threat group, likely state-sponsored, referenced in threat intelligence.
arbitrary commands concept
Any system commands that an attacker can execute without restriction, often leading to full compromise.
arbitrary Python code concept
Python commands or scripts that can be run without restriction, often resulting from a security vulnerability.
arbitrary-file-write vulnerability
Arbitrary-file-write is a vulnerability that lets an attacker write files to locations they should not control.
artificial intelligence concept
Artificial intelligence is technology that lets computers perform tasks that usually need human-like thinking or pattern recognition.
asyncrat
AsyncRAT is a remote access trojan that lets attackers control infected Windows computers and steal information.
attachment hash concept
An attachment hash is a short digital fingerprint used to identify an email attachment or file.
attack path mapping technique
The process of visualizing the possible routes an attacker could take through a network to reach a critical asset.
attack paths concept
Attack paths are the possible routes an attacker could take through systems, accounts, and weaknesses to reach a target.
attack surface concept
Attack surface is the total set of places where an attacker could try to enter or exploit a system.
attacker-controlled code execution technique
Attacker-controlled code execution means an attacker can make a system run code of their choosing.
attacks concept
Attacks are attempts to harm, break into, disrupt, or steal from computer systems or people using technology.
Austad's shop organization
A retailer (Austad's Golf) that may have been involved in a security incident.
authenticated attackers concept
Authenticated attackers are attackers who already have some valid login or access before exploiting a system.
authentication concept
Authentication is the process of proving that a person or system is allowed to log in.
authentication bypass vulnerability
Authentication bypass is a flaw or technique that lets someone access a system without properly logging in.
authentication bypass zero-days vulnerability
Previously unknown vulnerabilities that allow attackers to bypass authentication without valid credentials.
AutoHotKey tool
AutoHotKey is a Windows scripting tool used to automate keyboard, mouse, and other tasks.
AutoHotKey script tool
An AutoHotKey script is an automation script for Windows that can simulate keystrokes, mouse actions, and other tasks.
AWS product
AWS, or Amazon Web Services, is Amazon's cloud computing platform for hosting applications, storing data, and running online services.
Azure product
Azure is Microsoft's cloud computing platform for running apps, storing data, and managing online services.
Azure AD product
Azure AD, now called Microsoft Entra ID, is Microsoft’s cloud identity service for managing users, logins, and access.
Azure Sentinel product
Azure Sentinel, now called Microsoft Sentinel, is Microsoft’s cloud service for collecting security alerts and helping detect attacks.
backdoor malware
A backdoor is a hidden way into a system that lets someone bypass normal security controls.
backend infrastructure concept
The behind-the-scenes servers, databases, and networking that power a website or application.
BACnet protocol
BACnet is a communication protocol used by building automation systems such as HVAC, lighting, and access control.
Bajaj Auto organization
Bajaj Auto is an Indian automotive manufacturer known for motorcycles, scooters, and other vehicles.
Bajaj Auto Technology Limited organization
Bajaj Auto Technology Limited is a technology-related company associated with the Bajaj Auto business group.
Bashe threat-actor
A threat actor group referenced in security reporting.
Beacon Object Files tool
Beacon Object Files are small programs used with Cobalt Strike Beacon to run custom actions on a compromised computer.
belgium other
Belgium is a country in Western Europe.
Bishop Fox organization
Bishop Fox is a cybersecurity company known for offensive security testing and research.
bitdefender organization
Bitdefender is a cybersecurity company known for antivirus and other security products.
BitSight organization
A cybersecurity ratings company that provides risk monitoring and security performance metrics for organizations.
Black tool
The Python code formatter that enforces consistent code style.
Black tool
A code formatter for Python that enforces a consistent style.
Black Basta threat-actor
Black Basta is a ransomware and extortion group known for attacking organizations and stealing data.
black-hat-usa
Black Hat USA is a major cybersecurity conference where researchers present security findings and tools.
Block_layout concept
Block_layout is a low-level Objective-C block structure describing how a block of executable code and captured data is laid out in memory.
Blowfish protocol
Blowfish is an older encryption algorithm that uses a secret key to scramble and protect data.
blue-green strategy concept
A deployment technique that uses two identical environments to reduce downtime and risk when releasing new software.
BOD 26-04 directive concept
BOD 26-04 directive is a binding operational directive name, likely referring to a government cybersecurity requirement for agencies.
breach concept
A breach is an incident where someone gains unauthorized access to systems or data.
breach and attack simulation concept
An automated security testing platform that safely simulates real-world attacks to see how well an organization's defenses hold up.
BRIDGE:BREAK technique
A technique that breaks Layer-2 network bridging or isolation, potentially causing security boundary bypass.
Britain other
Britain is a common name for the United Kingdom or Great Britain, depending on context.
browser sandbox concept
A browser sandbox is a security feature that isolates web content so it has less ability to harm the rest of the computer.
Builder Code tool
Builder code is software code used to generate or package another program, such as creating a customized malware or agent build.
Bun tool
Bun is a tool for running and building JavaScript and TypeScript applications, similar to Node.js but designed to be fast.
business associates organization
Business associates are vendors or partners that handle protected health information for HIPAA-covered organizations.
business-email-compromise technique
Business email compromise is a scam where attackers use or impersonate business email accounts to trick people into sending money or data.
C++ concept
A widely-used programming language for building system software, applications, and games.
C2 concept
C2, short for command and control, is the system attackers use to send instructions to compromised devices or malware.
C2 server concept
A C2 server is a remote system used by attackers to manage malicious activity or collect stolen information.
caller ID concept
Caller ID is the phone feature that shows the number or name of the person calling.
CalPhishing Attacks technique
CalPhishing attacks are phishing attacks that abuse calendar invitations or scheduling features to trick victims.
cambodia other
Cambodia is a country in Southeast Asia that may be mentioned in cyber reports as a victim location, actor location, or operating base.
canada
Canada is a North American country often referenced in cybersecurity reporting as a target, regulator, or law-enforcement partner.
Canadian other
Canadian refers to something related to Canada.
carnival-corporation organization
Carnival Corporation is a major cruise company and travel organization.
Catalyst SD-WAN Manager product
Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager is a Cisco product used to centrally manage software-defined wide-area networks.
CDHash concept
CDHash is an Apple code-signing hash value used to identify signed code for trust and validation decisions.
CDHash kernel cache exploitation technique
CDHash kernel cache exploitation is a niche technique involving abuse of Apple code-signing hash data in low-level kernel cache behavior.
cdhash-cache-exploitation technique
cdhash-cache-exploitation refers to a niche exploitation technique involving cached code-signing hash behavior.
ceaseSystemExtensionWithReply: technique
A method on macOS that asks a system extension to stop, possibly used maliciously to disable security tools.
chief security officer concept
A chief security officer is a senior leader responsible for an organization’s security.
china
China is a country often referenced in cybersecurity reporting involving technology, espionage, regulation, and threat activity.
China-nexus group threat-actor
A China-nexus group is a group described as having a connection to China.
Chrome native messaging protocol protocol
The Chrome native messaging protocol lets a browser extension exchange messages with a program installed on the same computer.
churchoffice11011[at]gmail[.]com other
churchoffice11011[at]gmail[.]com is an email address written in a defanged form so it is not accidentally clicked or contacted.
CI concept
A software development practice where code changes are regularly merged and tested automatically.
CI runners concept
Programs that execute automated build and test jobs in a continuous integration pipeline, often on a server or cloud.
CI/CD concept
CI/CD is a software delivery approach that automatically builds, tests, and deploys code changes.
cisa organization
CISA is the U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency, which helps protect critical infrastructure and shares security guidance.
CISA guidance on BEC organization
CISA guidance on BEC is advice from the U.S. cybersecurity agency on preventing business email compromise scams.
CISA KEV concept
CISA KEV is the U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency's catalog of known exploited vulnerabilities that defenders should prioritize fixing.
CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog tool
The CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog is a CISA list of security flaws known to have been exploited.
CISA's KEV Catalog product
CISA's KEV Catalog is the U.S. cybersecurity agency’s list of known exploited vulnerabilities that organizations should urgently fix.
Cisco organization
Cisco is a major technology company that makes networking hardware, security products, and communications systems.
Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN product
Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN is Cisco's software-defined wide-area networking product for managing secure connections between sites and cloud services.
Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller product
A central management component for Cisco's SD-WAN solution that orchestrates policies and connectivity.
Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager product
Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager is Cisco's management product for configuring and monitoring Catalyst SD-WAN networks.
Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Validator product
A component of Cisco's SD-WAN solution that validates and authorizes device connections and configuration.
Cisco Unified CM Administration interface product
The Cisco Unified CM Administration interface is the web-based admin panel used to configure Cisco Unified Communications Manager.
Cisco Unified Communications Manager product
Cisco Unified Communications Manager is a Cisco product used to manage enterprise voice, video, messaging, and collaboration services.
Cisco Unified Communications Manager Session Management Edition product
Cisco Unified Communications Manager Session Management Edition is a Cisco product for managing call routing and session control across enterprise voice systems.
Cisco Unified Serviceability product
Cisco Unified Serviceability is a Cisco management interface used to monitor and troubleshoot Unified Communications services.
cisco-webdialer product
Cisco WebDialer is a Cisco calling feature that lets users place phone calls from a web page or application.
Citrix organization
Citrix is a company known for remote access, virtualization, and application delivery products.
claims concept
Requests made to an insurance company for payment or coverage of a loss.
claude-code
Claude Code is Anthropic’s AI coding assistant for helping developers write and manage software projects.
clawdbot tool
A software bot referenced in security reporting, likely used for automation or malicious purposes.
clearinghouses organization
Clearinghouses are organizations that process and route transactions or records between other parties, such as healthcare claims data.
clickfix
ClickFix is a social-engineering tactic that tricks users into copying and running malicious commands to “fix” a fake problem.
cloud computing account concept
An online user account that lets you access cloud services like storage, virtual machines, or email.
cloud control planes concept
Cloud control planes are the management layers that create, configure, and control cloud resources such as servers, networks, and permissions.
Cloud Detection and Response product
Cloud Detection and Response is security monitoring and response focused on attacks and suspicious activity in cloud environments.
cloud workloads concept
Applications or services running on cloud infrastructure, such as virtual machines or containers.
cloudflare
Cloudflare is a company that provides content delivery, DDoS protection, DNS, and web security services.
Cloudflare API product
The application programming interface for managing Cloudflare services like Workers, DNS, and security settings.
Cloudflare Queues product
A message queuing service by Cloudflare that enables reliable communication between distributed parts of an application.
Cloudflare Workers product
A serverless computing platform by Cloudflare that runs JavaScript or other code on the edge network.
Cloudflare Workers SDK tool
Cloudflare Workers SDK is a software development kit for building applications that run on Cloudflare’s serverless Workers platform.
CloudSEK organization
CloudSEK is a cybersecurity company that monitors online threats, leaked data, and criminal activity on the internet.
co-conspirators threat-actor
Co-conspirators are people who work together with others to plan or commit a crime.
co-op organization
Co-op is a British consumer cooperative and retail organization.
Cobalt Strike Beacon tool
Cobalt Strike Beacon is a powerful remote-control component of the Cobalt Strike testing tool that attackers also commonly abuse.
cobalt-strike
Cobalt Strike is a legitimate penetration-testing tool that attackers often abuse to control compromised systems.
code execution technique
Code execution means an attacker can make a computer or server run commands or software of their choosing.
code injection flaw vulnerability
A security vulnerability that allows an attacker to inject and execute malicious code within a vulnerable application.
codex
Codex is an OpenAI AI coding model or coding assistant technology for helping write and understand code.
codfish-semantic-release-action tool
codfish-semantic-release-action is a GitHub Action related to automating software releases with semantic-release.
colombia other
Colombia is a country in South America that may be referenced as a location for cyber incidents or targets.
command injection technique
Command injection is an attack where someone tricks an application into running system commands chosen by the attacker.
command injection flaw vulnerability
A security flaw that lets an attacker execute arbitrary commands on the host operating system through a vulnerable application.
command-and-control concept
Command-and-control is the way attackers remotely send instructions to malware and receive data back from infected machines.
command-and-control (C2) endpoint concept
A server or communication point used by attackers to send commands to compromised machines and receive stolen data.
command-and-control infrastructure concept
Command-and-control infrastructure is the servers and systems attackers use to send instructions to malware and receive stolen data.
command-line interface tool
A command-line interface is a text-based way to control a computer or program by typing commands.
composite action tool
A composite action is a GitHub Action that combines multiple workflow steps into one reusable action.
compromised concept
Compromised means a system, account, or device has been accessed or controlled by someone who should not have access.
compromised websites concept
Compromised websites are legitimate websites that attackers have broken into and changed or abused for malicious purposes.
computer intrusion concept
Computer intrusion is unauthorized access to a computer, account, or network.
confidential documents concept
Confidential documents are files meant to be kept private because they contain sensitive information.
configuration information concept
Settings and parameters that determine how software or a device behaves.
consent experience concept
The user interface and flow where a person grants permissions to an application during OAuth authorization.
conspiracy to commit computer intrusion concept
A criminal charge involving planning or agreeing to illegally access computers or networks.
consumer services concept
Consumer services are businesses that provide services directly to individual customers.
consumer spas concept
Customer-facing single-page web applications that run in the browser and dynamically update content.
containers concept
Containers are lightweight packages that run applications with their needed files in isolated environments.
Control Center - Feature Services product
Control Center - Feature Services is a Cisco administration area used to manage which communication features are running.
cookies concept
Cookies are small pieces of data websites store in a browser to remember logins, settings, or tracking information.
Cordyceps malware
A family of malicious software targeting macOS systems, used for spying or data theft.
covered entities organization
Covered entities are healthcare organizations, plans, or clearinghouses that must follow HIPAA privacy and security rules.
crafted branch name technique
A crafted branch name is a specially chosen source-code branch name designed to trigger a bug or security issue.
crafted file technique
A crafted file is a specially made file designed to trigger a bug, exploit a weakness, or test how software handles unusual input.
crafted HTTP request technique
A crafted HTTP request is a specially made web request designed to trigger a bug or unusual behavior in a server.
CrashFix tool
A tool or script that patches or fixes application crashes, possibly used to disguise malicious activity.
CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY concept
A PostgreSQL command that creates an index without locking writes, allowing concurrent data modifications.
credential rotation technique
Credential rotation means replacing passwords, keys, or tokens with new ones to reduce the risk from stolen or old credentials.
credential stuffing attacks technique
Credential stuffing attacks try stolen username-and-password pairs on many sites to break into accounts where people reused passwords.
credential theft technique
Credential theft is the stealing of usernames, passwords, tokens, or other login information.
credential-harvesting
Credential harvesting is collecting usernames, passwords, or login tokens from victims.
credential-stuffing technique
Credential stuffing is when attackers try stolen usernames and passwords on many websites to see where they still work.
credentials concept
Credentials are login details or access tokens that prove a user or system is allowed to access something.
crimeware tools tool
Crimeware tools are software tools made or used by criminals to steal data, break into systems, or run scams.
critical infrastructure concept
Critical infrastructure refers to essential systems and services that societies and organizations depend on.
CrowdStrike Falcon Sensor product
CrowdStrike Falcon Sensor is endpoint security software that monitors computers for malicious or suspicious activity.
crypto assets concept
Crypto assets are digital assets such as cryptocurrencies or tokens that use blockchain-related technology.
cryptocurrency concept
Cryptocurrency is digital money, such as Bitcoin, that uses cryptography and usually runs on a blockchain.
cryptocurrency accounts concept
Cryptocurrency accounts are wallets or exchange accounts used to hold, send, or receive digital assets.
cryptocurrency blockchains concept
Cryptocurrency blockchains are public digital ledgers that record cryptocurrency transactions.
cryptocurrency investment frauds concept
Cryptocurrency investment frauds are scams that trick people into sending digital money to fake or dishonest investment schemes.
cryptocurrency wallets product
Cryptocurrency wallets are tools used to store and manage access to digital currency funds.
CTI Services organization
CTI Services refers to cyber threat intelligence services that collect and analyze information about attackers, threats, and exposed data.
customer account numbers concept
Unique identifiers assigned to customers for their accounts in a business system.
customer database concept
A customer database is a stored collection of information about an organization's customers.
CVE vulnerability
A CVE is a public identifier used to track a specific known software vulnerability.
CVE-2024-21762 vulnerability
CVE-2024-21762 is a Fortinet FortiOS vulnerability that has been reported as exploited and can allow attackers to compromise affected devices.
CVSS concept
CVSS is a standard scoring system used to rate how severe a software vulnerability is.
cyber incident concept
A cyber incident is any event where digital systems, accounts, or data may have been attacked, exposed, or disrupted.
cyber insurance concept
Cyber insurance is insurance that helps cover costs from cyber incidents such as breaches, ransomware, or business disruption.
cyber resilience concept
Cyber resilience is an organization's ability to keep operating and recover quickly when cyberattacks or failures happen.
cyber scams concept
Cyber scams are online fraud schemes that use computers, phones, or the internet to trick people out of money or information.
cyberattack concept
A cyberattack is an attempt to damage, disrupt, or gain unauthorized access to computer systems or data.
cyberattack supply chain concept
A cyberattack supply chain is the chain of vendors, software, services, and dependencies an attacker may target to reach victims indirectly.
cybercrime as a service concept
Cybercrime as a service is when criminals sell ready-made hacking tools, access, or services to other criminals.
cybercrime forum concept
An online community where criminals share tools, sell stolen data, and discuss illegal hacking activities.
cybercrime group concept
A cybercrime group is an organized group that uses computers or the internet to commit crimes.
cybercrime underground concept
The cybercrime underground is the hidden online community where criminals trade stolen data, hacking tools, and illegal services.
Cybersecurity concept
Cybersecurity is the practice of protecting computers, networks, software, and data from digital attacks or misuse.
cybersecurity experts organization
Cybersecurity experts are people who specialize in protecting computers, networks, and data from attacks.
cybersecurity incident concept
A cybersecurity incident is an event that threatens the security of systems, networks, or data.
cybersecurity-and-infrastructure-security-agency organization
The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency is the U.S. government agency that helps defend critical infrastructure and improve cybersecurity.
DarkForums organization
DarkForums is an online forum name associated with cybercrime discussions and trading of illicit data or services.
data breach concept
A data breach is an incident where sensitive, private, or protected information is accessed, exposed, or stolen without permission.
data breaches concept
Data breaches happen when private or sensitive information is exposed, stolen, or accessed without permission.
data exfiltration technique
Data exfiltration is the unauthorized removal or copying of data from a system.
data security concept
Data security is the practice of protecting information from unauthorized access, theft, damage, or exposure.
data was stolen concept
Data was stolen means information was copied or taken by someone who was not authorized to have it.
data-theft-extortion technique
Data-theft-extortion is when criminals steal information and threaten to publish or sell it unless the victim pays.
dead-drop concept
A location used to secretly exchange items or information, leaving a package for another person to pick up later without direct contact.
decoys technique
Decoys are fake targets, files, accounts, or systems used to distract attackers or detect suspicious behavior.
deepfake-impersonation technique
Deepfake-impersonation is using AI-made fake audio, video, or images to make someone appear to say or do something they did not.
deepfake-powered impersonation technique
Deepfake-powered impersonation is the use of AI-generated audio, images, or video to pretend to be someone else.
Defused Cyber organization
Defused Cyber is a cybersecurity organization or service name associated with cyber threat intelligence and security research.
denmark other
Denmark is a country in Northern Europe.
detection script tool
A detection script is a small program used to check systems or files for signs of a threat or vulnerability.
Deutsche Bahn organization
Deutsche Bahn is Germany’s national railway company.
direct messages concept
Private, one-to-one communications sent within an online platform, like a chat or social media.
direct syscall technique
A method where malware invokes operating system functions directly rather than through the normal API to evade security monitoring.
direct-deposit concept
Direct-deposit is an electronic payment method that sends wages or benefits directly into a bank account.
direct-deposit-fraud concept
A scam where an attacker redirects payroll or benefit direct deposits to an account they control.
directory/path traversal vulnerability
Directory/path traversal is a vulnerability where attackers use manipulated paths to access files outside the intended folder.
dispatch_block_create tool
dispatch_block_create is an Apple developer function used to create a block of work for Grand Central Dispatch.
dispatchers other
Dispatchers are people who coordinate operations, such as sending trains, emergency responders, or workers where needed.
display-name-spoofing technique
Display-name-spoofing is making an email sender name look like a trusted person or company even when the real address is different.
disposable tooling concept
Disposable tooling is throwaway software or infrastructure attackers use briefly so it is harder to track or reuse against them.
DKIM protocol
DKIM is an email signing method that helps verify that a message was authorized by a domain and was not changed after signing.
DLL sideloading technique
DLL sideloading is an attack where malware places a fake DLL where a trusted Windows program will load it.
DllMain concept
The optional entry point function in a Windows DLL that is called when the DLL is loaded or unloaded, often used by malware to execute code.
DMARC protocol
DMARC is an email policy check that uses SPF or DKIM alignment to help determine whether a message claiming to come from a domain should be trusted.
docker
Docker is a platform for packaging and running applications in containers.
Docker-based action tool
A Docker-based action is a GitHub Action that runs inside a Docker container to perform automation tasks.
Dockerfile product
A Dockerfile is a text file with instructions for building a Docker image.
domains concept
Domains are human-readable internet names used to identify online locations.
Draft Guidance concept
Preliminary recommendations or rules published by an authority, often open for public comment before being finalized.
DraftKings organization
DraftKings is an online sports betting and fantasy sports company.
dragonforce
DragonForce is a ransomware group that extorts victims by stealing data and encrypting systems.
droppers malware
Droppers are malware programs whose main job is to install or download other malware onto a device.
DscCoreR.mui product
DscCoreR.mui is a Windows-style language resource file name that may be referenced in attacks if abused for loading or disguise.
dwell time concept
Dwell time is how long an attacker remains inside a victim’s environment before being detected or removed.
edge devices concept
Network devices like routers and firewalls that sit at the boundary between an internal network and the internet.
Edge Monitoring Agent product
Edge Monitoring Agent is a named software agent referenced in security reporting, likely used to monitor endpoint or network activity.
Edgecution malware
Edgecution is a named security issue or exploit involving Microsoft Edge, but I cannot define it confidently from the term alone.
EDR product
EDR is security software that monitors computers and servers for suspicious activity and helps respond to attacks.
EDS5000 product
EDS5000 is a Lantronix serial device server used to connect serial equipment to Ethernet networks.
EDS5000 version 2.2.0.0R1 product
EDS5000 version 2.2.0.0R1 is a specific firmware or software version for the Lantronix EDS5000 device server.
egress-filtering concept
The practice of monitoring and controlling data leaving a network to prevent data exfiltration.
elevate to root technique
The action of gaining the highest administrative privileges on a Unix/Linux system.
elevated privileges concept
Higher access rights than a standard user, allowing actions like installing software or modifying system files.
Elliptic organization
Elliptic is a company that analyzes cryptocurrency transactions to help detect fraud, sanctions evasion, and money laundering.
email authentication concept
Technical methods that verify an email came from the domain it claims and hasn't been forged.
email-spoofing technique
Email spoofing is when an attacker makes an email look like it came from someone else.
Emmenhtal malware
Emmenhtal is a malware loader used to deliver and run other malicious programs on infected systems.
endpoint detection and response product
Endpoint detection and response is security software that watches computers and devices for suspicious activity and helps respond to threats.
Endpoint Security Framework tool
Endpoint Security Framework is an Apple macOS framework that lets security tools monitor and respond to activity on a device.
energy concept
Energy is the sector that produces and delivers electricity, fuel, and related services.
entrypoint.js tool
entrypoint.js is a JavaScript file name commonly used for the main starting code of an application or package.
ephemeral instances concept
Ephemeral instances are temporary cloud or virtual machines that are created for short-term use and then destroyed.
EPSS concept
EPSS is a scoring system that estimates how likely a software vulnerability is to be exploited in the real world.
eraleign threat-actor
A threat actor group referenced in security reporting.
Errol Weiss other
Errol Weiss is a named person quoted in the article.
escrow services concept
Escrow services hold money or assets for two parties until agreed conditions are met, but criminal versions can help scams look trustworthy.
eset
ESET is a cybersecurity company that makes antivirus, endpoint protection, and threat research products.
ETW concept
Event Tracing for Windows, a built-in diagnostic and tracing facility used for security monitoring and often tampered with by attackers.
Eurojust organization
Eurojust is the European Union agency that helps countries cooperate on criminal justice investigations.
europe
Europe is a continent often referenced in cybersecurity reporting involving regulations, victims, threat activity, or law enforcement.
European rail operators organization
European rail operators are organizations that run passenger or freight railway services in Europe.
Europol organization
Europol is the European Union’s law enforcement agency that helps member countries fight serious and organized crime.
eval() technique
eval() is a programming function that runs text as code, which can be dangerous if attackers control the text.
evil-corp threat-actor
Evil Corp is a Russian cybercrime group associated with banking malware and ransomware operations.
evil_tenant.csv tool
evil_tenant.csv is a named example file, likely a crafted CSV used to demonstrate or test malicious tenant-upload behavior.
exfiltrated technique
Exfiltrated means data was secretly copied or moved out of a system by an attacker.
exposed credentials concept
Login details that have been accidentally published or stolen, granting unauthorized access.
exposed secret vulnerability
An exposed secret is a password, API key, or token accidentally made visible to people who should not have it.
exposure concept
The state of being at risk from a security threat, often referring to data or systems accessible to attackers.
exposure management concept
Exposure management is the process of finding, prioritizing, and reducing the ways an organization could be attacked.
face swapping technique
Face swapping is a technique that digitally replaces one person's face in an image or video with another person's face.
failed authentication attempts concept
Failed authentication attempts are unsuccessful tries to log in or prove identity to a system.
fake browser update prompts technique
Fake browser update prompts are deceptive pop-ups that trick users into installing malware by pretending to be browser updates.
fake instructional videos on TikTok technique
Social media videos designed to trick viewers into following malicious steps, like downloading malware disguised as a tutorial.
fake login screen technique
A fake login screen is a lookalike sign-in page used to trick people into entering their passwords.
fake Microsoft “Outlook Updates Management Console” technique
A fraudulent application or webpage mimicking a legitimate Microsoft management tool to trick users into installing malware.
fake-browser-updates
Fake browser updates are scam web pages that pretend a browser needs updating but actually install malware.
fakeupdates malware
FakeUpdates is another name for SocGholish, malware delivered through fake browser update prompts.
fbi organization
The FBI is the U.S. Federal Bureau of Investigation, which investigates cybercrime and national security threats.
FDA organization
The FDA, or U.S. Food and Drug Administration, is the U.S. agency that regulates food, drugs, and many medical devices.
FDA clearance concept
FDA clearance is a U.S. regulatory decision that allows certain medical devices to be marketed after review by the Food and Drug Administration.
fear, uncertainty, and doubt concept
Fear, uncertainty, and doubt is a tactic of spreading alarming or confusing claims to influence people's decisions.
Federal Civilian Executive Branch organization
Federal Civilian Executive Branch refers to U.S. federal civilian agencies that are part of the executive branch.
file-write payloads technique
File-write payloads are attack instructions or data designed to make a vulnerable system create or change files.
file:// protocol
A URL scheme that points to a file on your local computer, commonly used in browsers to access file systems.
FileFix technique
FileFix is a social-engineering attack technique that tricks users into pasting or running a malicious file path or command.
financial fraud concept
Financial fraud is deception used to steal money or gain an improper financial benefit.
FinCEN organization
The U.S. Treasury bureau that combats money laundering, terrorist financing, and other financial crimes.
finger.exe tool
A legacy TCP/IP utility on Windows that provides information about a user on a remote system, sometimes used for reconnaissance.
firewalls product
Firewalls are security systems that control which network traffic is allowed in or out.
firmware 2.1.0.0R3 product
Firmware 2.1.0.0R3 is a specific version of low-level software that runs on a hardware device.
first-contact addresses concept
First-contact addresses are email addresses or contact points attackers initially use to communicate with a victim.
Flare organization
Flare is a cybersecurity company that monitors dark web, cybercrime, and exposed-data sources for threats.
force-pushed technique
Force-pushed means a Git repository’s history was overwritten, which can replace commits others may have relied on.
Forescout Research Vedere Labs organization
The threat research division of Forescout Technologies, specializing in operational technology and IoT security.
Forescout Vedere Labs organization
The research arm of Forescout Technologies focusing on device and network security.
forked PR concept
A pull request submitted from a copy (fork) of a repository, commonly used in open-source contributions.
Fortinet FortiOS product
Fortinet FortiOS is the operating system that runs Fortinet firewall and network security devices.
france other
France is a country in Western Europe.
fraudulent activity concept
Fraudulent activity is dishonest or illegal behavior meant to deceive others for money, access, or advantage.
fraudulent investment platforms concept
Fraudulent investment platforms are fake websites or apps that trick people into putting money into nonexistent or rigged investments.
frontier agentic models concept
Frontier agentic models are highly capable AI models designed to plan and take actions with some autonomy.
fscan tool
fscan is a network scanning tool often used to discover hosts, services, and vulnerabilities in internal networks.
full write permissions concept
Access rights that allow a user or program to create, modify, and delete files or data on a system.
full-body ultrasound scanner product
A full-body ultrasound scanner is a medical imaging device that uses sound waves to scan large areas of the body.
full-body ultrasound study concept
A medical examination using ultrasound imaging to assess internal organs and tissues throughout the body.
GateKeeper .NET payload malware
A malicious .NET-based payload used in a malware family, possibly for loading additional modules or evading detection.
gcp product
GCP, or Google Cloud Platform, is Google's cloud computing service for running applications, storing data, and managing infrastructure online.
GenAI Chat Tools tool
GenAI chat tools are chatbot-style services that use generative AI to answer questions, write text, or help with tasks.
germany
Germany is a European country often referenced in cybersecurity reporting as a target, regulator, or law-enforcement partner.
git-tag-hijacking technique
An attack where an adversary changes the commit that a Git tag points to, potentially inserting malicious code into a repository.
GitHub product
GitHub is a platform for storing, managing, and collaborating on software code.
GitHub Action tool
A GitHub Action is an automation step or workflow component that runs tasks such as testing, building, or deploying code on GitHub.
GitHub Actions product
GitHub Actions is GitHub's automation service for building, testing, and deploying code when repository events happen.
GitHub API product
An application programming interface provided by GitHub for automating repository management, issue tracking, and user data access.
GitHub App key concept
A GitHub App key is a private credential that lets a GitHub App authenticate and access GitHub resources.
GitHub OIDC tokens concept
Short-lived tokens issued by GitHub via OpenID Connect to securely authenticate workflows to cloud providers without storing secrets.
GitHub's public commit search API tool
GitHub's public commit search API is an interface that lets users or programs search public GitHub code commits.
github-dead-drop-c2 technique
GitHub dead-drop C2 is a technique where attackers use GitHub content as a hidden place to store command-and-control instructions or addresses.
GITHUB_TOKEN concept
GITHUB_TOKEN is an automatic secret used by GitHub Actions to let workflows access the repository and related resources.
Gmail product
Gmail is Google’s email service.
Goat Shop other
Goat Shop is a named entity referenced in security reporting, likely as a service, site, or tool involved in a specific story.
Google organization
Google is a technology company that provides online services, including email-related services.
Google Cloud repository product
A Google Cloud repository is a code or artifact storage location hosted in Google Cloud services.
governance concept
Governance is the set of rules, oversight, and decision-making processes used to manage an organization or technology safely.
GPT 5.5-Cyber-Preview tool
An AI model, possibly a preview version of a GPT variant focused on cybersecurity tasks.
GPT-5.4-Cyber tool
An artificial intelligence model, possibly a variant of GPT, customized for cybersecurity tasks.
GraphQL API protocol
GraphQL API is a way for apps to request exactly the data they need from a service using the GraphQL query language.
group policy misconfigurations vulnerability
Group policy misconfigurations are incorrect Windows domain settings that can accidentally weaken security across many computers.
grpc
gRPC is a framework that lets software services communicate efficiently with each other over a network.
GSM-R protocol
GSM-R is a railway radio communication standard based on GSM technology and used for train operations.
GSM-R failures concept
Malfunctions in the GSM-R railway communication system that can disrupt safe train operations.
GSM-R outage concept
A loss of service on the GSM-R network, which is used for voice and data communication in railway operations.
H-Pay Service PLC organization
H-Pay Service PLC is a financial services company that has been linked in reporting and sanctions actions to suspicious payment and laundering activity.
hacked DraftKings accounts concept
Hacked DraftKings accounts are DraftKings user accounts that attackers accessed without permission.
hackers concept
Individuals who use technical skills to gain unauthorized access to computer systems, or security researchers who test defenses.
haowang-guarantee organization
Haowang Guarantee is another name associated with the HuiOne Guarantee marketplace ecosystem linked to illicit online services and scam payments.
hard-coded CI credentials vulnerability
Hard-coded CI credentials are passwords or tokens built directly into continuous-integration scripts or files, making them easy to steal if exposed.
Hargreaves Lansdown organization
Hargreaves Lansdown is a UK financial services company that provides investment and savings services.
harrods organization
Harrods is a luxury department store in London and a well-known retail organization.
have-i-been-pwned tool
A website that lets users check if their email addresses or passwords have appeared in known data breaches.
HaveIBeenPwned product
Have I Been Pwned is a website that lets people check whether their email address appeared in known data breaches.
Headless browser tool
A web browser that runs without a visible window, used by developers for automated testing and by attackers to scrape websites without being seen.
headless Edge browser tool
A headless Edge browser is Microsoft Edge running without a visible window, often controlled automatically by scripts or tools.
health plans organization
Health plans are insurance or healthcare coverage programs that pay for or manage medical care.
Health Sector concept
The part of the economy consisting of healthcare providers, insurers, and related organizations.
health-isac
Health-ISAC is an information-sharing group that helps healthcare organizations exchange cyber threat intelligence.
healthcare concept
Healthcare is the sector that provides medical care, hospitals, clinics, insurance, and related services.
Healthcare InfoSecurity organization
Healthcare InfoSecurity is a publication or organization focused on cybersecurity and privacy issues in healthcare.
healthcare organizations organization
Healthcare organizations are groups that provide, support, or manage healthcare services.
helios-ai other
helios-ai is a named entity referenced in security reporting, likely as an AI-related project, package, or account.
hexa-ai other
An AI service or platform referenced in security reporting, possibly related to code generation or analysis.
HIPAA concept
HIPAA is a U.S. law that sets privacy and security rules for protected health information.
hong-kong other
Hong Kong is a special administrative region of China often mentioned as a location in cyber or business reporting.
host-level executor concept
A software component that executes jobs directly on the host operating system, often in CI/CD pipelines.
HTTP requests protocol
HTTP requests are messages a browser or app sends to a web server to ask for a page, file, or action.
HTTP RPC module protocol
An HTTP RPC module is a software component that lets programs send remote commands or requests over HTTP.
Huione Group organization
A Cambodian conglomerate allegedly tied to large-scale online scam operations, including pig butchering fraud.
HuiOne Guarantee organization
HuiOne Guarantee is a Cambodia-linked online marketplace and escrow ecosystem reported to facilitate scams, money laundering, and illicit services.
human trafficking schemes concept
Human trafficking schemes are crimes where people are exploited or forced into work, scams, or other activities against their will.
Hydra product
Ory Hydra, an open-source OAuth 2.0 server for secure identity and authorization management.
Hydra consent session API product
An API provided by Ory Hydra to manage user consent flows during OAuth 2.0 authorization.
Hydra service product
The OAuth 2.0 server component of Ory, responsible for issuing and managing access tokens.
IaaS concept
A cloud service model providing virtualized computing resources over the internet, like storage and virtual machines.
IBM X-Force organization
IBM's internal cybersecurity intelligence and threat research team.
identity concept
Identity is the set of information that proves who a user, device, or service is in a system.
if: always() concept
A condition used in GitHub Actions workflows that ensures a job runs even if previous steps failed, which can be exploited in pipeline attacks.
imposter commit attack technique
An imposter commit attack is when an attacker makes a code commit appear to come from a trusted person or project identity.
improper access control vulnerability
A weakness where a system fails to properly restrict user access, allowing unauthorized actions or data exposure.
improper input validation vulnerability
Improper input validation is a software flaw where a program fails to properly check data it receives, which can let attackers cause harmful behavior.
incident response concept
Incident response is the process of investigating and handling a security problem after it is found.
InCrease tool
A utility or malware tool referenced in security reporting, possibly used for code injection or privilege escalation.
index.js product
index.js is a common JavaScript file name often used as the main entry point for a program or package.
india
India is a South Asian country often referenced in cybersecurity stories involving technology, government, and large organizations.
indonesia
Indonesia is a Southeast Asian country that may be mentioned in cybersecurity reporting as a location, target, or government context.
infostealers malware
Infostealers are malware designed to steal passwords, cookies, cryptocurrency data, or other sensitive information from a device.
initial access broker threat-actor
An initial access broker is a cybercriminal who breaks into networks and then sells that access to other attackers.
initial infection technique
Initial infection is the first step where malware or an attacker gains a foothold on a device or network.
initial-access brokers threat-actor
Initial-access brokers are criminals who break into organizations and sell that access to other attackers.
Insufficient Egress Filtering concept
A security weakness where outbound network traffic is not adequately filtered, allowing data leaks or malicious communications.
Interlock threat-actor
Interlock is a ransomware or extortion group name used in cybercrime reporting.
internal APIs concept
Internal APIs are software interfaces meant to be used only inside an organization or application, not by the public.
internal developer platforms concept
Platforms built by organizations to provide self-service tools and environments for their developers.
internal spearphishing technique
A targeted phishing attack that originates from within an organization's own compromised accounts to trick colleagues.
IP addresses protocol
IP addresses are numerical internet addresses used to locate computers, servers, or other networked systems.
IRONSCALES Adaptive AI product
IRONSCALES Adaptive AI is an email security product feature that uses artificial intelligence to detect and respond to phishing threats.
IT concept
IT, or information technology, is the use and management of computers, networks, and software in an organization.
IT experts other
IT experts are people who specialize in setting up, maintaining, and fixing computer systems and networks.
IT glitches concept
Minor temporary faults or errors in information technology systems.
jailbreaking technique
Jailbreaking is removing built-in restrictions from a device so the user can run unauthorized software or change protected settings.
JavaScript for Automation tool
JavaScript for Automation is an Apple macOS feature that lets JavaScript control apps and automate system tasks.
jitasm tool
A just-in-time assembler library that generates machine code at runtime, sometimes used in evasion.
Joseph Garrison threat-actor
Joseph Garrison is a person referenced in security or legal reporting.
Kamerin Stokes threat-actor
Kamerin Stokes is a person referenced in security or legal reporting.
Kandji MDM Agent product
Kandji MDM Agent is Kandji's device-management agent software used to help administer and secure Apple devices.
key-exchange protocol
Key exchange is the process two systems use to safely agree on encryption keys before communicating securely.
kill switch concept
A kill switch is a built-in way to quickly stop software, malware, or a system from continuing to operate.
Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog concept
A U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency list of vulnerabilities that are actively being exploited in the wild.
known-exploited vulnerabilities vulnerability
Known-exploited vulnerabilities are security flaws that attackers have already been seen using in real attacks.
KongTuke threat-actor
A threat actor group referenced in security reporting, possibly related to advanced operations.
KongTuke/Woodgnat threat-actor
A threat actor group referenced in security reporting, possibly associated with advanced persistent threats.
LabKit tool
A testing and analysis tool, possibly associated with GitLab, used for developing and debugging extensions.
Lantronix organization
Lantronix is a technology vendor that makes networking, connectivity, and remote access devices.
Lantronix EDS5000 product
A line of serial-to-IP converter devices from Lantronix, used to connect serial equipment to a network.
Lantronix EDS5000 Series devices product
Serial-to-Ethernet converters by Lantronix for connecting legacy serial equipment to an IP network.
lateral movement technique
Lateral movement is moving from one connected system to another after gaining access somewhere.
law enforcement organization
Law enforcement means police or government agencies responsible for investigating and stopping crime.
lebanon other
Lebanon is a country in the Middle East.
legitimate administration tools concept
Tools designed for system administrators that can be misused by attackers for malicious purposes because they are trusted.
legitimate banking sector concept
The legitimate banking sector means regulated banks and financial institutions that operate legally.
LLM concept
An LLM, or large language model, is an AI system trained on large amounts of text to understand and generate language.
LLM agents concept
LLM agents are AI systems that use large language models to plan actions and use tools on a user’s behalf.
LLM misconfigurations concept
Errors in the setup or deployment of large language models that can lead to security vulnerabilities or unintended behavior.
lockbit malware
LockBit is a major ransomware group and ransomware-as-a-service operation known for encrypting and stealing victim data.
Log4Shell vulnerability
Log4Shell is the widely exploited Log4j vulnerability that allowed remote code execution in many Java applications.
logic flaws vulnerability
Logic flaws are mistakes in how software is designed to make decisions, letting someone misuse a feature even if the code does not crash.
login credentials concept
Information like usernames and passwords used to access accounts.
Lumma Stealer malware
Lumma Stealer is malware that steals passwords, browser cookies, cryptocurrency wallets, and other sensitive data.
M365 product
M365 is a common abbreviation for Microsoft 365, Microsoft’s cloud-based email, office, and collaboration service suite.
macOS product
macOS is Apple’s operating system for Mac computers.
macOS product
Apple's desktop operating system for Mac computers.
malformed headers concept
Malformed headers are incorrectly structured metadata fields in network requests, files, or messages that may confuse software or expose bugs.
malicious code concept
Malicious code is software instructions written to harm systems, steal data, or give attackers control.
malicious URL concept
A malicious URL is a web link designed to lead people to malware, phishing pages, or other harmful content.
malloc/free tool
malloc/free are C programming functions used to allocate and release memory manually.
malware concept
Malware is software made to harm, spy on, or take control of computers or data.
malware-as-a-service concept
Malware-as-a-service is a business model where criminals rent or sell malware tools and infrastructure to other attackers.
mandiant
Mandiant is a cybersecurity company known for incident response, threat intelligence, and breach investigations.
manufacturing concept
Manufacturing is the industry that makes physical goods, such as cars, electronics, or machinery.
Marks & Spencer organization
A major British multinational retailer, often referenced in breach or security incident reporting.
marks-and-spencer organization
Marks & Spencer is a major British retailer that has been referenced in cybersecurity news as a victim organization.
MCP protocol
MCP, or Model Context Protocol, is a standard way for AI assistants to connect to external tools, files, and services.
MCP clients product
MCP clients are apps or agents that connect to servers using the Model Context Protocol to access tools, data, or context.
medical device security concept
Medical device security is the practice of protecting connected healthcare equipment from hacking, misuse, or unsafe changes.
medical research concept
Medical research is scientific work aimed at understanding health, disease, treatments, or related medical topics.
medical-device development concept
Medical-device development is the process of designing, building, testing, and approving healthcare equipment or software.
MFA bypass technique
MFA bypass is any method attackers use to get around multi-factor authentication protections.
Miasma tool
Miasma is a phishing kit or campaign name used in cybercrime reporting.
microsoft organization
Microsoft is a major technology company that makes Windows, Office, Azure, and many enterprise security products.
Microsoft Azure product
Microsoft Azure is Microsoft's cloud computing platform.
Microsoft Detours tool
Microsoft Detours is a developer library for intercepting and redirecting Windows function calls.
Microsoft Digital Defense Report 2024 other
Microsoft Digital Defense Report 2024 is Microsoft's annual report describing major cyber threats and security trends seen by the company.
Microsoft Edge extension product
A small add-on program that extends the functionality of the Microsoft Edge web browser.
Microsoft Entra ID product
Microsoft Entra ID is Microsoft's cloud identity service for managing users, logins, apps, and access permissions.
microsoft-365
Microsoft 365 is Microsoft’s cloud productivity and security suite including Office apps, email, storage, and collaboration tools.
microsoft-edge product
Microsoft Edge is Microsoft’s web browser.
microsoft-exchange
Microsoft Exchange is Microsoft’s email and calendar server software used by many organizations.
microsoft-sharepoint
Microsoft SharePoint is Microsoft’s document collaboration and intranet platform.
microsoft-teams
Microsoft Teams is Microsoft’s workplace chat, meeting, and collaboration platform.
Midjourney product
Midjourney is an AI image-generation service that creates images from text prompts.
MinHook tool
MinHook is a small Windows library used to hook, or intercept, function calls in software.
misconfiguration vulnerability
Misconfiguration is an unsafe or incorrect setting that can expose a system to attack.
Mistic threat-actor
A threat actor group or malware referenced in security reporting.
mitiga
Mitiga is a cloud incident response and threat detection company.
mitigation efforts concept
Actions taken to reduce the impact or likelihood of a security threat or vulnerability.
MITRE ATT&CK T1534 technique
MITRE ATT&CK T1534 is the ATT&CK technique for internal spearphishing, where attackers use messages inside an organization to trick users.
mitre-attack-t1534 technique
MITRE ATT&CK T1534 is the ATT&CK technique for internal spearphishing, where attackers send phishing messages inside a compromised organization.
mitre-attack-t1598 technique
MITRE ATT&CK T1598 is the technique for phishing for information, where attackers trick people into revealing useful details.
MITRE: T1598 technique
MITRE T1598 is the ATT&CK technique for phishing for information, where attackers try to trick people into revealing useful data.
Mobile Device Management product
Mobile Device Management is software used by organizations to configure, monitor, and secure phones, tablets, and computers.
Mock Mythic server tool
A mock Mythic server is a test server that imitates Mythic so researchers can develop or test code without using a real deployment.
Modbus protocol
Modbus is an industrial communication protocol used by machines and control systems to exchange data.
model tampering technique
Model tampering is changing an AI or machine-learning model to make it behave incorrectly, leak data, or favor an attacker.
ModeloRAT malware
ModeloRAT is a named remote access trojan malware family reported in threat research.
moltbot malware
A bot or malware family referenced in security reporting, likely used for automated attacks or data exfiltration.
money laundering concept
The illegal process of making money obtained from crime appear to come from a legitimate source.
money laundering services concept
Money laundering services help criminals disguise where illegally obtained money came from.
MpExtMs.exe product
MpExtMs.exe is a Windows executable name that may be legitimate or may be abused by attackers to disguise or launch malicious activity.
mtlbackdoor malware
A backdoor malware family referenced in security reporting.
multi-factor authentication concept
Multi-factor authentication is a login method that asks users to prove who they are in more than one way.
Mythic tool
Mythic is an open-source command-and-control framework used by red teams to manage agents during security testing.
Mythic Agent tool
A Mythic Agent is a program used with the Mythic command-and-control framework to run tasks on a compromised machine.
Mythic implant development tool
The creation of custom payloads for the Mythic command-and-control framework used in red teaming or malicious operations.
Mythic RPC Messages protocol
Mythic RPC Messages are internal remote-procedure-call messages used by Mythic components to communicate tasks and results.
mythic-cli tool
mythic-cli is a command-line tool used to manage the Mythic command-and-control framework.
Mythicd tool
Mythicd is the main background service that runs and coordinates the Mythic command-and-control framework.
Nathan Austad threat-actor
Nathan Austad is a person referenced in security reporting.
native desktop applications concept
Native desktop applications are programs installed directly on a computer and built for that operating system.
nepal other
Nepal is a country in South Asia.
netherlands
The Netherlands is a European country often mentioned in cybersecurity stories involving hosting, law enforcement, or victims.
networks concept
Connected systems and devices that can communicate and share resources, like the internet or a corporate LAN.
next-generation railway communications platform product
A next-generation railway communications platform is a newer system intended to replace older railway radio or data communication technology.
NIB payload injection technique
NIB payload injection is a technique that abuses Apple NIB interface files to insert or trigger unwanted code or behavior.
Node.js runtimes concept
The environment that executes JavaScript code on the server side, enabling building web applications.
north-macedonia other
North Macedonia is a country in southeastern Europe.
Novee Security organization
A cybersecurity company referenced in security reporting, possibly providing threat intelligence or security services.
npm product
npm is the main package manager and public registry for JavaScript software libraries.
NPM_TOKEN concept
NPM_TOKEN is a secret access token used to authenticate to the npm package registry for publishing or managing packages.
NSXPC protocol
