NSXPC is an Apple framework for structured communication between separate processes on macOS and related systems.
OAuth protocol
An authorization protocol that allows a user to grant a third-party application limited access to their resources without sharing their password.
OAuth phishing attacks technique
Phishing attempts that trick users into granting permissions to a malicious application using OAuth, without revealing their password.
OAuth policy data concept
Information about rules and permissions that govern how OAuth tokens and access are managed.
obfuscated JavaScript technique
Obfuscated JavaScript is JavaScript code intentionally made hard to read so its purpose is harder to understand.
objc_msgSend tool
objc_msgSend is a low-level Objective-C runtime function used to call methods on objects.
objective-c-runtime-bridge tool
objective-c-runtime-bridge appears to be a tool or technique for interacting with the Objective-C runtime from another environment.
Okta organization
Okta is a company that provides identity and login management services for organizations.
online marketplaces concept
Online marketplaces are websites or platforms where buyers and sellers trade goods, services, or digital items.
OpenClaw product
OpenClaw is a named tool or project, but I cannot define it confidently from the term alone.
OpenCode tool
OpenCode is an AI-assisted coding tool or project name used to help write or edit software code.
operating system commands concept
Instructions given to an operating system, like 'dir' on Windows or 'ls' on Linux, to perform actions.
operation-endgame
Operation Endgame is an international law-enforcement action aimed at disrupting major malware and cybercrime infrastructure.
Opus 4.6 tool
A tool or software version referenced in security reporting, possibly related to malware command-and-control.
oracle
Oracle is a technology company known for databases, enterprise software, cloud services, and Java-related products.
Ory organization
A technology company that provides open-source identity and access management solutions like Hydra and Kratos.
Ory Hydra product
An open-source OAuth 2.0 and OpenID Connect server built by Ory for secure identity management.
OS commands concept
Commands typed into a command-line interface to interact with an operating system.
OSAKit.framework tool
OSAKit.framework is an Apple framework related to scripting and automation support on macOS.
OT concept
OT, or operational technology, is hardware and software that monitors or controls physical equipment like factory machines or power systems.
out-of-band verification technique
Out-of-band verification means confirming a request through a separate communication channel, such as calling a known phone number instead of replying to an email.
Outlook product
Outlook is Microsoft's email and calendar application used by individuals and organizations.
oven-sh/setup-bun tool
oven-sh/setup-bun is a GitHub Action that installs the Bun JavaScript runtime in automated workflows.
p=none policy protocol
A p=none policy is a DMARC email setting that monitors spoofed mail but does not block or quarantine it.
PaaS concept
A cloud computing model where a provider offers a platform allowing customers to develop, run, and manage applications without maintaining infrastructure.
password reset concept
The process of changing a user's password, typically after they've forgotten it or it's been compromised.
password resets concept
The process of changing or recovering a password, often used when a user forgets their current password.
passwords concept
Passwords are secret words or strings used to prove you are allowed to access an account or device.
patch cycle concept
A patch cycle is the regular schedule an organization follows to apply software security updates.
path-traversal vulnerability
Path traversal is a vulnerability where attackers use file path tricks to access files they should not be able to reach.
payload concept
A payload is the part of malicious code that performs the attacker’s intended action.
payment methods under their control concept
Financial accounts or payment instruments that an attacker owns and can use to receive fraudulent payments.
Payouts Kings ransomware operation threat-actor
A cybercriminal group that uses ransomware to extort money from organizations, promising payouts to affiliates.
payouts-king malware
payouts-king refers to the Payouts King ransomware family.
payroll redirect BEC technique
Payroll redirect BEC is a business email compromise scam that tricks a company into sending an employee’s pay to the attacker’s bank account.
payroll-redirect technique
Payroll-redirect refers to fraud where an attacker changes an employee’s payroll details so wages go to the attacker’s account.
People Data Labs organization
People Data Labs is a company that provides large datasets of personal and professional profile information.
Perfect DLL Hijacking technique
Perfect DLL Hijacking is a DLL hijacking method where a malicious replacement DLL closely matches what the legitimate program expects.
permissions concept
Permissions are specific allowed actions, such as viewing information or making changes.
Personal Access Tokens concept
Personal access tokens are secret strings that let apps or users access an online service without using a password directly.
personal and financial data concept
Personal and financial data is information about a person and their money, such as names, addresses, account numbers, or card details.
Phil Englert other
Phil Englert is a person referenced in security reporting.
Philipp Nagl other
A security researcher or individual referenced in security reporting.
phishing campaigns technique
Phishing campaigns are organized attempts to trick many people into revealing information or installing malware using fake messages.
phishing websites technique
Phishing websites are fake sites designed to trick people into entering passwords, payment details, or other sensitive information.
Picus organization
A cybersecurity company that provides automated breach and attack simulation to help organizations test their defenses continuously.
pig-butchering technique
Pig-butchering is a long-running scam where criminals build trust with a victim before tricking them into fake investments.
pillager tool
Pillager is a security tool used to collect credentials and sensitive information from compromised systems.
pivoted technique
Pivoted means an attacker used one compromised system as a stepping stone to reach another system or network.
PlanetScale organization
A database platform company offering a serverless MySQL-compatible database service.
plymouth other
Plymouth is a city in southern England.
powershell
PowerShell is Microsoft’s command-line shell and scripting language, often used by administrators and abused by attackers.
PowerShell script tool
A PowerShell script is a text file with commands that automate tasks on Windows systems.
PPID spoofing technique
A technique where malware fakes the parent process ID to make a malicious process appear to have been started by a legitimate program.
precautionary measures concept
Steps taken in advance to prevent potential security incidents or reduce their impact.
Prince Group organization
Prince Group is a Cambodia-linked business group that authorities have associated with large-scale online scam operations and related criminal networks.
private data concept
Sensitive information that should not be publicly disclosed, like personal identifiers or financial records.
privilege-escalation technique
Privilege escalation is when an attacker gains higher levels of access than they were supposed to have.
privilege-escalation vulnerability vulnerability
A security flaw that allows an attacker with limited access to gain higher-level permissions on a system.
privileged XPC methods technique
XPC interfaces that require elevated privileges to call, often targeted by attackers to abuse inter-process communication on Apple platforms.
prompt injection technique
Prompt injection is a way of giving an AI system instructions that may make it behave in unintended ways.
proof-of-concept technique
A working demonstration, often a piece of code, that proves a security vulnerability can actually be exploited.
Proofpoint organization
Proofpoint is a cybersecurity company known for email security, threat intelligence, and data protection products.
proprietary messaging platforms concept
Private, non-public messaging systems like custom chat apps.
providers that bill insurance concept
Healthcare or service providers that submit claims directly to insurance companies for payment.
public relations concept
Public relations is the work of managing how an organization communicates with the public and media.
public services concept
Services provided by government to citizens, such as healthcare or transportation.
pull requests concept
Pull requests are proposals to merge code changes into a software project, commonly used on platforms like GitHub.
Python tool
Python is a popular programming language often used to write scripts, automation tools, and security research code.
Python Software Foundation organization
The Python Software Foundation is the nonprofit organization that supports the Python programming language and its community.
Python version 3.13.3 product
Python version 3.13.3 is a specific release of the Python programming language.
Python-based backdoor malware
A Python-based backdoor is malicious software written in Python that gives an attacker secret access to a computer.
QA sub-agent concept
An agentic tool that specializes in testing software or validating outputs as part of a larger AI system.
qilin
Qilin is a ransomware group that steals data and encrypts victim systems for extortion.
RabbitMQ tool
RabbitMQ is message-broker software that lets different parts of an application send messages to each other reliably.
railway communications network product
A railway communications network is the system that lets trains, dispatchers, and railway equipment exchange operational messages.
ransom concept
A sum of money demanded by criminals to restore access to data or systems after a ransomware attack.
ransom demand concept
A ransom demand is a message from criminals asking for payment, usually to unlock systems or stop leaked data from being published.
ransomware concept
Ransomware is malicious software or an operation that locks or steals data to pressure victims into paying money.
ransomware attack concept
A ransomware attack is when criminals lock files or systems and demand payment to restore access or prevent data leaks.
ransomware campaigns concept
Ransomware campaigns are organized efforts using software that locks or disrupts systems to demand payment.
ransomware gangs threat-actor
Criminal groups that use malicious software to encrypt victims' data and demand payment for its release.
RDP protocol
RDP is Microsoft’s Remote Desktop Protocol, which lets someone control a Windows computer over a network.
redhat-cloud-services
Red Hat Cloud Services are cloud-based Red Hat tools and managed services for enterprise software and infrastructure.
RedLine Stealer malware
RedLine Stealer is malware that steals saved passwords, browser cookies, cryptocurrency data, and other personal information.
Reflective loading technique
A technique for loading a DLL from memory without using the normal Windows loader, often used to avoid detection by security software.
refresh token concept
A refresh token is a login token that lets an app get new access tokens without making the user sign in again.
refresh token coalescing concept
A technique to combine multiple refresh token requests into one to reduce overhead or hide malicious behavior.
refresh token grace period concept
A short time window after a refresh token is used where it can be retrieved or reused before being invalidated.
Registry Run key technique
A Registry Run key is a Windows setting attackers can abuse to make a program start automatically when a user logs in.
regulatory filing concept
A regulatory filing is an official document an organization submits to a government or regulator.
remote access sessions concept
Connections that allow a user to control a computer from a different location, like via RDP or SSH.
remote code execution chain technique
A series of vulnerabilities and exploits combined to achieve remote code execution on a target system.
remote-code-execution technique
Remote code execution is a serious flaw that lets an attacker run commands on a system from elsewhere.
repos concept
Repos are repositories, meaning storage locations for source code, project files, or software packages.
repositories concept
Repositories are places where software project files and related development data are stored.
reused login credentials concept
Reused login credentials are the same username and password used across multiple sites, which makes account takeover easier if one site is breached.
revocation concept
The process of invalidating a digital credential or certificate so it can no longer be used.
RevokeAndItGoesKaboom tool
A proof-of-concept tool or technique that demonstrates a vulnerability by crashing something after token revocation.
Rhysida threat-actor
Rhysida is a ransomware and extortion group known for attacks on organizations including public-sector and healthcare targets.
rogue root accounts concept
Unauthorized superuser accounts created by attackers to maintain persistent access to a system.
rogue-peering concept
Illegitimate network interconnections, often created to intercept or manipulate traffic between networks.
roles concept
Sets of permissions assigned to users or systems that define what actions they can perform.
root access concept
Having complete administrative control over a computer or network, allowing unrestricted system changes.
root-level command injection vulnerability
An injection flaw that allows an attacker to run commands with the highest system privileges, potentially taking full control.
RSA_SHA256_signature protocol
RSA_SHA256_signature is a digital signature method that uses RSA and SHA-256 to prove data came from a trusted signer and was not changed.
runProcessWithCommand: technique
A function or method that executes a shell command with specified arguments, used by both legitimate software and malware.
runZero product
runZero is a cybersecurity product used to discover and inventory devices connected to networks.
russia
Russia is a country frequently referenced in cybersecurity stories involving cybercrime groups, espionage, and government activity.
S-Bahn product
S-Bahn is a commuter rail system used in German-speaking countries.
S7comm protocol
S7comm is an industrial protocol used by Siemens S7 programmable logic controllers to communicate.
SaaS concept
SaaS is software delivered over the internet as a subscription or hosted service instead of installed and managed entirely by the customer.
SaaS integrations concept
SaaS integrations are connections that let cloud-based software services share data or automate work with each other.
salesforce product
Salesforce is a cloud platform for customer relationship management, sales, service, and business applications.
sandbox concept
A sandbox is an isolated environment used to run code more safely so it cannot easily affect the rest of the system.
sanitization technique
Sanitization is cleaning or filtering input or data so it cannot cause harmful behavior in a system.
scam compound operators threat-actor
Criminal groups that oversee large-scale scam operations, often from physical locations where forced labor occurs.
Scattered Spider threat-actor
Scattered Spider is a cybercrime group known for social engineering, account theft, and high-profile intrusions.
scheduled replacement concept
A planned process of replacing hardware, software, or credentials at a specific time for security or maintenance.
scheduled task technique
A scheduled task is a job set to run automatically at a certain time or under certain conditions.
SD-WAN Manager web interface product
The web-based management console for controlling and configuring an SD-WAN solution.
SD-WAN peering connections concept
Network links established between SD-WAN devices to exchange routing information and traffic securely.
SD-WAN templates concept
Predefined configuration patterns for software-defined wide area network devices that simplify deployment.
SDK concept
A collection of tools, libraries, and documentation that developers use to build applications for a specific platform.
searchall tool
A command or utility that searches and lists files or data, possibly used in post-exploitation.
SecCodeCheckValidity() tool
SecCodeCheckValidity() is an Apple Security framework function used to check whether code satisfies signing and validity requirements.
security products concept
Software or hardware designed to protect systems from cyber threats, like antivirus or firewalls.
security updates concept
Security updates are software fixes that close vulnerabilities or improve protection against threats.
segmentation illusion concept
Segmentation illusion is the mistaken belief that networks are separated safely when hidden connections or rules still let attackers move between them.
SELECT * concept
A SQL statement that retrieves all columns from a database table, commonly used in queries.
semantic-release tool
semantic-release is a software tool that automates versioning and publishing releases based on commit messages.
serbia
Serbia is a country in Southeast Europe that may appear in cybersecurity reporting as a location or target.
serial-to-ethernet servers product
Serial-to-Ethernet servers are devices that connect older serial equipment to modern Ethernet networks.
serial-to-IP converters product
Devices that allow serial devices to communicate over a network, converting RS-232/485 signals to IP protocol.
server-side-request-forgery vulnerability
Server-side request forgery is a vulnerability that lets an attacker make a server send requests to places the attacker should not be able to reach directly.
serverless concept
Serverless is a cloud model where developers run code without managing the underlying servers directly.
servers tool
Servers are computers that provide services, websites, or data to other computers over a network.
service desk social engineering technique
Service desk social engineering is tricking help desk staff into changing accounts, resetting passwords, or revealing information.
service provider organization
A service provider is a company or organization that delivers a service, such as hosting, software, payments, or IT support, to customers.
service-level agreement concept
A contract that defines the expected service performance and uptime between a provider and a customer.
ServiceNow product
ServiceNow is a cloud platform companies use to manage IT support, workflows, and business services.
session tokens concept
Session tokens are temporary digital values that prove a user is already logged in to a website or service.
shadow IT concept
Shadow IT is technology used inside an organization without official approval or oversight from the IT department.
shadow-ai
Shadow AI is the use of AI tools in an organization without official approval, visibility, or security oversight.
SharkLoader malware
SharkLoader is a named malware loader used to deliver or start other malicious payloads.
shell command concept
A text instruction given to a command-line interpreter to perform an operation, such as listing files or running a program.
shell commands concept
Shell commands are text instructions typed into a command line to tell a computer what to do.
side-load technique
Side-load means to make a program load a file or component from an unexpected place, often so malicious code runs under a trusted program.
SIEM product
A security tool that gathers and analyzes logs from across an organization in real time to spot suspicious activity.
signaling systems product
Signaling systems are communications systems that coordinate and control operations such as trains, calls, or network services.
sigstore tool
Sigstore is a toolset for digitally signing and verifying software so users can trust where it came from.
silent-ransom-group
Silent Ransom Group is a cyber extortion group known for social engineering and data theft attacks.
Silex malware
A destructive malware that wipes firmware on IoT devices, rendering them inoperable.
SIM-swapped phones concept
SIM-swapped phones are phones whose numbers have been fraudulently transferred to a SIM card controlled by someone else.
sim-swapping technique
SIM swapping is a fraud technique where criminals move a victim’s phone number to a SIM card they control.
sinkholing technique
Sinkholing is redirecting malicious internet traffic to a controlled server so defenders can study or block it.
slack
Slack is a workplace messaging and collaboration platform.
Sleep technique
A function that pauses program execution for a specified time, sometimes used by malware to slow down analysis or avoid heuristic detection.
SmokeLoader malware
SmokeLoader is a malware family commonly used to download and install other malware on infected computers.
snoopy other
snoopy is a named entity referenced in security reporting, likely as a tool, alias, or software component.
SOAR product
SOAR, or security orchestration, automation, and response, is software that helps security teams automate investigations and response tasks.
SOC organization
SOC stands for security operations center, a team or function that monitors and responds to security issues.
socgholish malware
SocGholish is malware commonly delivered through fake browser update pages.
social engineering technique
Social engineering is manipulation of people into doing something or sharing information they otherwise would not.
SOCKS proxy protocol
A SOCKS proxy is a server that relays network traffic between a user and the internet, often to hide the user’s real location.
source code concept
The human-readable code written by programmers that makes up software.
Southeast Asian scam centers concept
Southeast Asian scam centers are operations in parts of Southeast Asia where people are often coerced into running online fraud schemes.
southeast-asia other
Southeast Asia is a region of Asia often referenced when describing where cyber activity or victims are located.
southern England other
Southern England is the southern region of England in the United Kingdom.
spam filter update product
A spam filter update is a change to software rules or data that helps identify and block unwanted or malicious messages.
Specops Password Policy product
Specops Password Policy is a product that helps organizations enforce stronger password rules in Microsoft Active Directory.
Specops Secure Service Desk product
Specops Secure Service Desk is a product that helps verify users securely during help desk password resets and support requests.
Specops Software organization
A company that provides password management and authentication solutions for enterprises.
SPF protocol
SPF is an email check that verifies whether a sending server is allowed to send email for a domain.
SQL migrations concept
Controlled changes to a database's structure, often managed by version control.
SSD Secure Disclosure organization
SSD Secure Disclosure is a vulnerability disclosure company that reports security flaws to vendors and publishes advisories.
SSH protocol
SSH is a secure way to remotely log in to and control another computer over a network.
ssh-lateral-movement technique
SSH lateral movement is when an attacker uses SSH access to move from one computer to another inside a network.
ssrf vulnerability
A type of web vulnerability where an attacker tricks a server into making unintended requests to internal systems or external sites.
stage-0 implant concept
An extremely lightweight initial payload that is deployed first, often just retrieving the next stage.
Stage-1 capability concept
The first, lightweight piece of malware used to gain a foothold on a target system before deploying further payloads.
standard input/output data streams concept
Standard input/output data streams are the basic channels programs use to receive text input and send text output.
state-sponsored hacking groups threat-actor
State-sponsored hacking groups are attacker teams supported or directed by a government.
stealc
StealC is information-stealing malware that targets passwords, browser data, cryptocurrency wallets, and other sensitive information.
StepSecurity Harden-Runner tool
StepSecurity Harden-Runner is a GitHub Actions security tool that monitors and restricts workflow network and file activity.
stolen accounts concept
User accounts that have been taken over by attackers, often through credential theft or phishing.
stolen credential concept
A stolen credential is a password, token, or other login secret that an attacker has obtained.
stolen credentials concept
Stolen credentials are usernames, passwords, or login tokens that attackers have obtained without permission.
stolen login credentials concept
Stolen login credentials are usernames, passwords, tokens, or keys taken by attackers and used to access accounts.
StrikeShark malware
StrikeShark is a named malware or attack tool reported in threat research.
supply-chain-attacks technique
Supply-chain-attacks are attacks that target the software or service creation process so many downstream users can be affected.
svchost.exe concept
A legitimate Windows process that hosts services, often abused by malware to blend in with normal system activity.
Symantec organization
Symantec is a cybersecurity company known for antivirus, endpoint protection, and threat intelligence products.
SyncRes.dat concept
A data file that stores DNS resolution information, sometimes used by malware to log network queries.
syria other
Syria is a country in the Middle East.
systems concept
Systems are organized sets of hardware, software, or processes working together.
SystemSettings.dll product
SystemSettings.dll is a Windows-related dynamic link library name that can be abused if a malicious file with that name is loaded instead of the legitimate one.
SystemSettings.exe product
SystemSettings.exe is a legitimate Windows program used to open the Windows Settings app, but attackers may abuse similarly named files to hide malware.
tag hijacking technique
Tag hijacking is taking over or changing a software version tag so users receive different code than expected.
tags concept
Labels or metadata attached to resources to categorize and organize them.
taiwan other
Taiwan is a territory in East Asia that is often referenced in cybersecurity reporting involving regional targeting or geopolitical activity.
Tasking Handler Code tool
Tasking handler code is code that receives, processes, or sends instructions for an agent in a command-and-control system.
Tata Electronics organization
A subsidiary of the Tata Group that manufactures electronic components and products.
technical component concept
A piece of hardware or software that forms part of a larger system.
technical failure concept
A technical failure is a malfunction in hardware, software, or infrastructure that prevents something from working correctly.
technical team organization
A technical team is a group of people responsible for engineering, IT, or other technology work.
technology concept
Technology refers to tools, systems, and software used to process information or perform tasks.
telegram
Telegram is a messaging app that supports channels, groups, bots, and encrypted chats.
Telegram-based marketplace concept
An online black market hosted on the Telegram messaging app, often used to sell stolen data, malware, or illegal services.
Telehealth and Telecare Aware organization
Telehealth and Telecare Aware is an organization or service focused on awareness of remote healthcare and care technology.
tenable
Tenable is a cybersecurity company known for vulnerability scanning and exposure management tools.
Tenable One Exposure Management Platform product
Tenable One Exposure Management Platform is a product suite that helps organizations see and prioritize security weaknesses across their environments.
tenable-one product
Tenable One is Tenable’s exposure management platform for finding, prioritizing, and reducing cybersecurity risks.
terminateAppsAndAgents: technique
A method used by malware to stop running applications and security agents on a compromised system.
the-mfn-plug other
A referenced entity, possibly a Telegram channel or tool used for cybercrime.
TheBeautifulSandsOfTime threat-actor
A threat actor group or campaign referenced in security reporting.
thebeautifulsnadsoftime other
thebeautifulsnadsoftime is a named entity referenced in security reporting, likely as an alias, account, tool, or campaign name.
third-party AI wrappers product
Third-party AI wrappers are outside services or libraries that package access to AI models behind their own interface.
Third-Party Identity and Access Management concept
Third-Party Identity and Access Management is the process of controlling how outside vendors, partners, or contractors log in to an organization's systems.
threat actor threat-actor
A threat actor is the person, group, or organization carrying out a cyberattack.
Threat actors concept
Threat actors are people or groups that carry out harmful activity against computers, websites, or organizations.
threat hunt concept
The proactive process of searching through networks and data to find hidden threats that automated tools missed.
threat hunting workflows concept
Structured processes and methodologies used by security teams to conduct proactive searches for threats.
threat intelligence concept
Threat intelligence is information collected and analyzed about possible or active cyber threats.
threat model concept
A threat model is a structured way to think about what could attack a system, how, and what should be protected.
token concept
A digital piece of data used for authentication, authorization, or as a session identifier.
tokens concept
Digital objects used for authentication, identification, or authorization, like access tokens.
tor
Tor is a privacy network that routes internet traffic through multiple relays to hide a user’s location.
Tor data leak site concept
A Tor data leak site is a hidden website, often run by extortion groups, where stolen victim data is posted or threatened for release.
train drivers other
Train drivers are the people who operate trains.
transaction details concept
Information about a financial transaction, including amounts, parties, and timestamps.
transducers product
Transducers are device components that convert one form of energy into another, such as ultrasound probes converting electrical signals into sound waves.
Transnational Criminal Organization concept
A Transnational Criminal Organization is a criminal group that operates across national borders.
troot other
troot is a named entity referenced in security reporting, likely as a tool, alias, or malware-related component.
Trump’s AI Executive Order other
Trump’s AI Executive Order refers to a U.S. presidential order setting government policy around artificial intelligence.
trusted application components concept
Parts of an application that are considered safe and are relied upon for security, like signed libraries.
U.S. authorities organization
Federal government agencies in the United States, such as the FBI or CISA, responsible for enforcing laws and cybersecurity.
U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency organization
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency is the U.S. government agency that helps protect critical infrastructure and improve cybersecurity.
U.S. Department of Justice organization
The U.S. Department of Justice is the U.S. government department responsible for federal law enforcement and legal prosecutions.
U.S. Treasury's Financial Crimes Enforcement Network organization
A bureau of the U.S. Treasury that collects and analyzes financial transactions to combat money laundering and other financial crimes.
ubiquiti organization
Ubiquiti is a company that makes networking equipment such as routers, wireless access points, and cameras.
Ubiquiti UniFi OS product
An operating system for Ubiquiti network devices that provides management and monitoring interfaces.
Ubiquiti UniFi OS product
An operating system for Ubiquiti network devices, known for its web management interface.
unauthenticated attacker concept
An attacker who does not have valid login credentials and attempts to access systems without authorization.
unauthenticated, remote attacker threat-actor
An unauthenticated, remote attacker is someone who can exploit a system over a network without first logging in.
underground marketplaces concept
Hidden websites or forums where illegal goods and services, like stolen data or hacking tools, are bought and sold.
underlying operating system concept
The core system software on which applications and services run, like Windows or Linux.
underwriting concept
The process of evaluating risk to determine insurance coverage and pricing.
UniFi OS product
UniFi OS is Ubiquiti’s operating system for managing UniFi networking devices and services.
united-kingdom
The United Kingdom is a country often referenced in cybersecurity reporting as a target, regulator, or law-enforcement partner.
united-states
The United States is a country often referenced in cybersecurity stories as a target, source, regulator, or law-enforcement actor.
unmanaged model dependencies vulnerability
Unmanaged model dependencies are external AI model components or libraries that are not properly tracked, updated, or secured.
US other
US refers to the United States.
us-department-of-justice organization
The U.S. Department of Justice is the federal government department responsible for law enforcement and legal actions, including cybercrime cases.
us-treasury organization
us-treasury is a shorthand reference to the U.S. Treasury, the U.S. government department involved in finance, sanctions, and financial-crime enforcement.
username concept
A unique identifier chosen by a person or system to log into an account or system.
username parameter concept
A field in a login request or database query that holds a user's account name.
v2 concept
Version two of a system, protocol, or software, indicating a major iteration.
v3 concept
Version three of a system or standard.
v4 concept
A version number, often referring to the fourth iteration of a standard or protocol, such as IPv4.
v5 concept
Version five of a system or protocol.
VA OIG organization
VA OIG is the Office of Inspector General for the U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs, which audits and investigates VA programs.
Vectored Exception Handler technique
A Windows mechanism that processes exceptions, sometimes hijacked by malware to intercept and handle errors for code execution.
vendor email compromise technique
Vendor email compromise is when attackers take over or impersonate a supplier’s email account to trick a business into paying or sharing information.
verifications-io organization
Verifications.io was an email verification service that became known after a large exposed database of personal information was found online.
Verizon Data Breach Investigations Report organization
The Verizon Data Breach Investigations Report is an annual report that summarizes real-world data breaches and attack trends.
Verizon DBIR 2026 other
Verizon DBIR 2026 would refer to Verizon's 2026 Data Breach Investigations Report, an annual report on breach and incident trends.
verizon-dbir product
Verizon DBIR is Verizon's annual Data Breach Investigations Report that summarizes cybercrime and breach trends.
Verizon’s Data Breach Investigation Report other
Verizon’s Data Breach Investigation Report is an annual report that summarizes real-world security incidents and data breaches.
version tags concept
Labels in a version control system that mark specific points in history, like release versions.
version.dll product
version.dll is a legitimate Windows library name often abused in DLL sideloading because programs may try to load it automatically.
Vidar Stealer malware
Vidar Stealer is malware that steals browser data, passwords, cryptocurrency wallets, and other sensitive information.
VirtualAlloc technique
A memory allocation function in Windows often used by malware to prepare code execution.
vishing technique
Vishing is phishing done by voice calls, where scammers try to trick people into revealing information or taking actions.
vManage product
vManage is the Cisco SD-WAN management component used to configure and monitor SD-WAN deployments.
vManage-admin concept
vManage-admin is likely an administrative account, role, or component associated with Cisco vManage management access.
VMs concept
VMs, or virtual machines, are software-based computers that run inside a physical computer or cloud server.
VNC protocol
VNC is remote-control software that lets someone view and operate another computer’s desktop over a network.
voice-cloning technique
Voice-cloning is using technology to create speech that sounds like a specific person's voice.
VoIP services protocol
VoIP services let people make phone calls over the internet instead of traditional phone lines.
vSmart product
vSmart is the Cisco SD-WAN controller component that helps manage routing and policy decisions.
vulnami concept
A term used in security reporting, possibly a vulnerability name or exploit tool.
vulnerability remediation concept
The process of fixing or mitigating security flaws to remove the risk they pose.
WannaCry malware
WannaCry is a famous ransomware worm from 2017 that spread rapidly by exploiting a Windows vulnerability.
Weak Outbound Controls concept
Insufficient restrictions on data leaving a network, making it easier for attackers to exfiltrate information.
weak passwords concept
Weak passwords are easy-to-guess passwords that make accounts easier for attackers to break into.
weaponization concept
Weaponization is the stage where a tool, exploit, or weakness is turned into something that can be used in an actual attack.
WebDialer service product
A service that allows web-based initiation of phone calls, sometimes exploited for fraud.
webshells malware
Webshells are malicious scripts placed on web servers that let attackers remotely control the server through a browser or web request.
Windows batch script tool
A Windows batch script is a text file containing commands that Windows can run automatically.
Windows loader lock concept
The Windows loader lock is an internal Windows mechanism that controls safe loading and unloading of DLLs in a process.
windows-11
Windows 11 is Microsoft’s modern desktop operating system.
WinPython tool
WinPython is a portable distribution of the Python programming language for Windows.
wire concept
A wire is an electronic bank transfer used to move money between accounts.
wireless communications outage concept
A wireless communications outage is a failure that stops radio or mobile communication systems from working properly.
Wiz organization
A cloud security company that helps organizations identify and remediate risks in their cloud environments.
Woodgnat threat-actor
An alias used by a threat actor group, likely part of the KongTuke/Woodgnat cluster.
wordpress product
WordPress is a popular content management system used to build websites and blogs.
workarounds concept
Temporary fixes or alternative methods to reduce a security risk until a permanent patch is available.
World Leaks threat-actor
World Leaks is a data-leak extortion group or leak site name associated with publishing stolen data.
Wrangler tool
Cloudflare's command-line tool for developing and deploying Workers and Pages projects.
write access concept
Write access means permission to create, change, or delete data in a system.
X product
X is a social media platform formerly known as Twitter.
XPC Hunter tool
XPC Hunter is a security tool or research project referenced in relation to analyzing Apple XPC behavior.
XPC services concept
Inter-process communication services on macOS and iOS that allow applications to securely exchange messages and delegate tasks.
xpc_connection_set_peer_code_signing_requirement() tool
xpc_connection_set_peer_code_signing_requirement() is an Apple XPC function used to require that a connecting process meet a code-signing rule.
zero-click attacks technique
Zero-click attacks are attacks that can compromise a device without the victim clicking anything.
zero-day attacks technique
Zero-day attacks exploit security flaws before the vendor has released a fix or before defenders widely know about them.
zero-days vulnerability
Zero-days are software vulnerabilities that attackers know about before the vendor has released a fix.
ZIP archive concept
A ZIP archive is a compressed file that can contain one or more files or folders.
Zscaler organization
A cybersecurity company that provides secure web gateways, zero-trust access, and cloud-delivered security services.

No matching terms.
